data.title: Retail sales in the US stalled at the end of the year, with a flat growth in December compared to November. Economists predicted a 0.4% gain but sales remained unchanged. The trend shifted from steady growth, with a noticeable slowdown in the last few months. Categories like furniture, electronics, and restaurants saw a decline in sales, but necessity-driven categories held. Questions arise about consumer strength as credit card debt surges and inflation squeezes budgets.
data.description: Retail sales unexpectedly stalled at the end of the year, casting doubt on the American consumer's strength after years of being a key driver of economic growth. The Commerce Department reported that December sales were flat compared to November, which was short of economists' expectations of a 0.4% increase. The trend of steady growth throughout the year broke, with previous months seeing mixed results. Categories like furniture, electronics, and restaurants experienced sales declines, while necessities such as building materials, gas stations, and food stores held steady. This signaled a shift in consumer behavior towards more selective spending. The news suggests possible implications for the economy as uncertainty looms about the future spending habits of consumers.

data.title: Stock market saw Dow rise, Nasdaq fall; vaccine stock surged. Dow closes higher, Nasdaq slips in choppy session.
data.description: In today's stock market session, the Dow Jones index maintained a positive trend after initial fluctuations, ultimately closing higher. Conversely, the Nasdaq index finished the day in negative territory. One notable highlight was a vaccine-related stock experiencing a significant surge in value. The overall market performance was described as choppy, with the Dow ending on a positive note and the Nasdaq slipping. Additionally, a travel company stock continued its downward trajectory.

data.title: MakeMyTrip partners with OpenAI to enhance AI capabilities for smarter travel discovery, aims to improve customer engagement.
data.description: MakeMyTrip Limited (NASDAQ: MMYT) has partnered with OpenAI to boost its AI capabilities in travel discovery. The integration of OpenAI's APIs into its app will improve travel planning by providing structured options and seamless booking. MakeMyTrip's AI-driven Myra interface handles over 50,000 daily conversations in multiple languages. This initiative aims to enhance user engagement, especially in Tier-2 cities where 45% of queries originate. The collaboration enhances MakeMyTrip's competitive edge in the travel industry. Leveraging AI is crucial for streamlining the travel planning process and improving customer engagement. This strategic move highlights the company's commitment to growth.

data.title: Dow Jones index drops, Astera and Robinhood plunge on earnings; Nasdaq-100 rallies. Vertiv sparks AI, chip stock surge.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index erased gains from Wednesday following a surprising January jobs report. Astera and Robinhood experienced declines in earnings. Notably, Vertiv drove significant buying activity in AI and chip stocks, resulting in a rally for the Nasdaq-100 while the Dow showed a cooling trend.

data.title: PPL Corporation reports 2025 earnings; EPS growth 7.1% over 2024. 2026 forecast: $1.90-$1.98 per share; $23B infra investment plan from 2026-2029; Dividend increased to $0.2850 per share.
data.description: In the news article, PPL Corporation reported 2025 earnings at $1.59 per share (GAAP), and earnings from ongoing operations were $1.81 per share, marking a 7.1% growth from 2024. The company projected a 2026 earnings forecast range of $1.90 to $1.98 per share, with a midpoint of $1.94 per share, representing a 7.2% growth over 2025 ongoing earnings. PPL extended its annual EPS growth target of 6% to 8% through 2029 and updated capital plans to $23 billion for infrastructure investments by 2029, aiming for an average annual rate base growth of around 10.3%. Quarterly common stock dividend increased to $0.2850 per share. Additionally, the company highlighted operational efficiencies, investment strategies, and growth opportunities in various segments to achieve sustainable energy solutions. PPL aims to ensure utility bills remain affordable while focusing on utility advancements and customer programs. The company's strong performance and strategic initiatives are positioning it well for future growth and shareholder returns.

data.title: Fidelity National Financial (NYSE: FNF) declares $0.52 quarterly dividend per share of common stock; payable on March 31, 2026.

data.title: TDS reports strong financial results for 2025, sets goal of 2.1M fiber service addresses. Teleconference on Feb. 20th at 9:00am CST.
data.description: TDS Telecom has raised its target for marketable fiber service to 2.1 million addresses, as revealed in their 2026 guidance alongside Array. A teleconference is scheduled for February 20, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. CST to elaborate on this development.
